contents.value:
```go
func make(t Type, size ...IntegerType) Type
```
Allocates and initializes an object of type slice, map, or chan (only). Like `new`, the first argument is a type, not a value. Unlike `new`, `make`'s return type is the same as the type of its argument, not a pointer to it. For slices, the size specifies the length; a second integer specifies the capacity.

```go
func new(Type) *Type
```
Allocates memory for a value of the specified type, initializes it to the zero value, and returns a pointer to it.

```go
func append(slice []Type, elems ...Type) []Type
```
Appends elements to the end of a slice. If the slice has sufficient capacity, the destination is resliced to accommodate the new elements. If it does not, a new underlying array is allocated.

```go
func copy(dst, src []Type) int
```
Copies elements from a source slice into a destination slice. Returns the number of elements copied, which is the minimum of `len(src)` and `len(dst)`. Also supports copying from a string into `[]byte`.

```go
func delete(m map[Type]Type1, key Type)
```
Deletes the element with the specified key from the map. If the map is nil or there is no such element, `delete` is a no-op.

```go
func len(v Type) int
```
Returns the length of `v` according to its type: number of elements in an array, pointer to array, slice, map, or channel buffer; number of bytes in a string.

```go
func cap(v Type) int
```
Returns the capacity of `v` according to its type: maximum length a slice can reach when resliced; length of the underlying array; or buffer capacity of a channel.

```go
func close(c chan<- Type)
```
Closes the channel, indicating that no more values will be sent on it. Only the sender should close a channel, never the receiver. Sending to or closing an already-closed channel causes a panic.

```go
func panic(v any)
```
Stops normal execution of the current goroutine. When a function F calls `panic`, normal execution of F stops immediately. Any deferred functions run, then F returns to its caller, which treats the call as a `panic`.

```go
func recover() any
```
Allows a program to manage behavior of a panicking goroutine. Executing a call to `recover` inside a deferred function stops the panicking sequence and returns the value passed to `panic`. Outside deferred functions, `recover` always returns `nil`.

```go
func complex(r, i FloatType) ComplexType
```
Constructs a complex value from two floating-point values. The real and imaginary parts must be of the same size (`float32` → `complex64`, `float64` → `complex128`).

```go
func real(c ComplexType) FloatType
```
Returns the real part of the complex number `c`. The return type is `float32` for `complex64` arguments, and `float64` for `complex128`.

```go
func imag(c ComplexType) FloatType
```
Returns the imaginary part of the complex number `c`. The return type is `float32` for `complex64` arguments, and `float64` for `complex128`.

```go
func clear[T ~[]Type | ~map[Type]Type1](t T)
```
(Go 1.21+) For maps, deletes all entries, resulting in an empty map. For slices, sets all elements up to the length to the zero value of the element type.

```go
func min[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns the smallest value among its arguments. Works with any ordered type (integers, floats, strings).

```go
func max[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns the largest value among its arguments. Works with any ordered type (integers, floats, strings).

```go
func print(args ...Type)
```
Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way and writes the result to standard error. Useful for bootstrapping and debugging; not guaranteed to stay in the language. Use `fmt` package for production code.

```go
func println(args ...Type)
```
Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way, adds spaces between arguments, and writes a newline to standard error. Use `fmt.Println` for production code.

```go
type string
```
The set of all strings of 8-bit bytes, conventionally but not necessarily representing UTF-8-encoded text. A string may be empty, but not nil. Values of string type are immutable.

```go
type int
```
A signed integer type that is at least 32 bits in size. It is a distinct type, however, and not an alias for, say, `int32`. The size is platform-dependent: 32 bits on 32-bit systems, 64 bits on 64-bit systems.

```go
type float64
```
The set of all IEEE-754 64-bit floating-point numbers. The default floating-point type in Go.

```go
type bool
```
The set of boolean values, `true` and `false`. The zero value is `false`.

```go
type byte = uint8
```
An alias for `uint8`. Conventionally used to distinguish byte values from 8-bit unsigned integer values.

```go
type rune = int32
```
An alias for `int32`. Conventionally used to distinguish character values from integer values. Represents a Unicode code point.

```go
type any = interface{}
```
(Go 1.18+) An alias for `interface{}`. Can hold a value of any type.

```go
type comparable interface{ comparable }
```
(Go 1.18+) An interface implemented by all comparable types (booleans, numbers, strings, pointers, channels, arrays of comparable types, structs whose fields are all comparable types). Used as a type constraint.

```go
func fmt.Println(a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Formats using the default formats for its operands and writes to standard output. Spaces are always added between operands and a newline is appended. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered.

```go
func fmt.Printf(format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Formats according to a format specifier and writes to standard output. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered.

```go
func fmt.Sprintf(format string, a ...any) string
```
Formats according to a format specifier and returns the resulting string.

```go
func fmt.Errorf(format string, a ...any) error
```
Formats according to a format specifier and returns the string as a value that satisfies `error`. Use `%w` to wrap an error so it can be unwrapped with `errors.Is` / `errors.As`.

```go
func fmt.Fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Formats according to a format specifier and writes to `w`. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered.

```go
func fmt.Sscanf(str string, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
```
Scans the argument string, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments as determined by the format. Returns the number of items successfully scanned.

```go
func os.Open(name string) (*os.File, error)
```
Opens the named file for reading. If successful, methods on the returned `File` can be used for reading; the associated file descriptor has mode `O_RDONLY`.

```go
func os.Create(name string) (*os.File, error)
```
Creates or truncates the named file. If the file already exists, it is truncated. If it does not exist, it is created with mode 0666 (before umask). The `File` is opened for writing.

```go
func os.ReadFile(name string) ([]byte, error)
```
Reads the named file and returns the contents. A successful call returns `err == nil`, not `err == io.EOF`.

```go
func os.WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm os.FileMode) error
```
Writes data to the named file, creating it if necessary. If the file does not exist, `WriteFile` creates it with permissions `perm`; otherwise it truncates it before writing.

```go
func os.Exit(code int)
```
Causes the current program to exit with the given status code. Conventionally, code zero indicates success, non-zero an error. The program terminates immediately; deferred functions are not run.

```go
func os.Getenv(key string) string
```
Retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. It returns the value, which will be empty if the variable is not present.

```go
func os.Setenv(key, value string) error
```
Sets the value of the environment variable named by the key.

```go
func os.MkdirAll(path string, perm os.FileMode) error
```
Creates a directory named path, along with any necessary parents, and returns `nil`, or else returns an error.

```go
func os.Remove(name string) error
```
Removes the named file or (empty) directory. If there is an error, it will be of type `*PathError`.

```go
func io.ReadAll(r io.Reader) ([]byte, error)
```
Reads from `r` until an error or EOF and returns the data it read. A successful call returns `err == nil`, not `err == io.EOF`.

```go
func io.Copy(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader) (written int64, err error)
```
Copies from `src` to `dst` until either EOF is reached on `src` or an error occurs. Returns the number of bytes copied and the first error encountered while copying, if any.

```go
func io.NopCloser(r io.Reader) io.ReadCloser
```
Returns a `ReadCloser` with a no-op `Close` method wrapping the provided `Reader r`.

```go
func strings.Contains(s, substr string) bool
```
Reports whether `substr` is within `s`.

```go
func strings.HasPrefix(s, prefix string) bool
```
Reports whether the string `s` begins with `prefix`.

```go
func strings.Split(s, sep string) []string
```
Slices `s` into all substrings separated by `sep` and returns a slice of the substrings between those separators.

```go
func strings.Join(elems []string, sep string) string
```
Concatenates the elements of its first argument to create a single string. The separator string `sep` is placed between elements.

```go
func strings.TrimSpace(s string) string
```
Returns a slice of the string `s`, with all leading and trailing white space removed, as defined by Unicode.

```go
func strings.Replace(s, old, new string, n int) string
```
Returns a copy of the string `s` with the first `n` non-overlapping instances of `old` replaced by `new`. If `n < 0`, there is no limit on the number of replacements.

```go
type strings.Builder struct { ... }
```
A `Builder` is used to efficiently build a string using `WriteString` and `Write` methods. It minimizes memory copying. The zero value is ready to use. Do not copy a non-zero Builder.

```go
func strconv.Atoi(s string) (int, error)
```
Equivalent to `ParseInt(s, 10, 0)`, converted to type `int`. Parses a string as a decimal integer.

```go
func strconv.Itoa(i int) string
```
Equivalent to `FormatInt(int64(i), 10)`. Returns the string representation of `i`.

```go
func strconv.FormatFloat(f float64, fmt byte, prec, bitSize int) string
```
Converts the floating-point number `f` to a string according to the format `fmt` and precision `prec`. The `bitSize` is 32 for `float32` and 64 for `float64`.

```go
func strconv.ParseInt(s string, base int, bitSize int) (int64, error)
```
Interprets a string `s` in the given base (0, 2 to 36) and bit size (0 to 64) and returns the corresponding value.

```go
func json.Marshal(v any) ([]byte, error)
```
Returns the JSON encoding of `v`. Struct fields are encoded using the struct field name or the `json` tag if present.

```go
func json.Unmarshal(data []byte, v any) error
```
Parses the JSON-encoded data and stores the result in the value pointed to by `v`. If `v` is nil or not a pointer, `Unmarshal` returns an error.

```go
func json.NewEncoder(w io.Writer) *json.Encoder
```
Returns a new encoder that writes to `w`. Call `Encode(v)` to write the JSON encoding of `v` to the stream, followed by a newline character.

```go
func json.NewDecoder(r io.Reader) *json.Decoder
```
Returns a new decoder that reads from `r`. Call `Decode(&v)` to decode the next JSON value from the input and store it in `v`.

```go
type json.RawMessage []byte
```
A raw encoded JSON value. It implements `Marshaler` and `Unmarshaler` and can be used to delay JSON decoding or precompute a JSON encoding.

```go
func http.ListenAndServe(addr string, handler http.Handler) error
```
Listens on the TCP network address `addr` and then calls `Serve` with `handler` to handle requests on incoming connections. If `handler` is `nil`, `DefaultServeMux` is used.

```go
func http.HandleFunc(pattern string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request))
```
Registers the handler function for the given pattern in the `DefaultServeMux`.

```go
func http.Get(url string) (*http.Response, error)
```
Issues a GET to the specified URL. Caller should close `resp.Body` when done reading from it.

```go
func http.NewRequest(method, url string, body io.Reader) (*http.Request, error)
```
Returns a new `Request` given a method, URL, and optional body. Use `http.NewRequestWithContext` to associate a context.

```go
func context.Background() context.Context
```
Returns a non-nil, empty `Context`. It is never canceled, has no values, and has no deadline. It is typically used by the main function, initialization, and tests, and as the top-level Context for incoming requests.

```go
func context.WithCancel(parent context.Context) (ctx context.Context, cancel context.CancelFunc)
```
Returns a copy of parent with a new Done channel. The returned context's Done channel is closed when the returned cancel function is called or when the parent context's Done channel is closed.

```go
func context.WithTimeout(parent context.Context, timeout time.Duration) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc)
```
Returns `WithDeadline(parent, time.Now().Add(timeout))`. The cancel function releases resources, so code should call cancel as soon as operations running in this Context complete.

```go
func context.WithValue(parent context.Context, key, val any) context.Context
```
Returns a copy of parent in which the value associated with `key` is `val`. Use context Values only for request-scoped data that transits processes and API boundaries.

```go
func context.WithDeadline(parent context.Context, d time.Time) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc)
```
Returns a copy of the parent context with the deadline adjusted to be no later than `d`. If the parent's deadline is already earlier, the returned context is semantically equivalent to parent.

```go
type sync.Mutex struct { ... }
```
A mutual exclusion lock. The zero value for a `Mutex` is an unlocked mutex. A `Mutex` must not be copied after first use. Call `Lock()` and `Unlock()` to acquire and release the lock.

```go
type sync.WaitGroup struct { ... }
```
A `WaitGroup` waits for a collection of goroutines to finish. The main goroutine calls `Add` to set the number of goroutines to wait for. Each goroutine calls `Done` when finished. `Wait` blocks until all goroutines have finished.

```go
type sync.Once struct { ... }
```
An object that will perform exactly one action. Call `Do(f)` to invoke `f` only once, even if called from multiple goroutines.

```go
type sync.Map struct { ... }
```
A `Map` is like a Go `map[any]any` but safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines without additional locking or coordination. Methods: `Store`, `Load`, `Delete`, `Range`, `LoadOrStore`, `LoadAndDelete`.

```go
type sync.RWMutex struct { ... }
```
A reader/writer mutual exclusion lock. The lock can be held by an arbitrary number of readers or a single writer. Methods: `RLock`, `RUnlock`, `Lock`, `Unlock`.

```go
func errors.New(text string) error
```
Returns an error that formats as the given text. Each call to `New` returns a distinct error value even if the text is identical.

```go
func errors.Is(err, target error) bool
```
Reports whether any error in `err`'s tree matches `target`. An error is considered a match if it is equal to the target or if it implements `Is(error) bool` such that `Is(target)` returns true.

```go
func errors.As(err error, target any) bool
```
Finds the first error in `err`'s tree that matches `target`, and if one is found, sets `target` to that error value and returns true.

```go
func errors.Join(errs ...error) error
```
(Go 1.20+) Returns an error that wraps the given errors. Any nil error values are discarded. Returns nil if every value in errs is nil.

```go
func errors.Unwrap(err error) error
```
Returns the result of calling the `Unwrap` method on `err`, if `err`'s type contains an `Unwrap` method returning `error`. Otherwise, `Unwrap` returns nil.

```go
func time.Now() time.Time
```
Returns the current local time.

```go
func time.Since(t time.Time) time.Duration
```
Returns the time elapsed since `t`. It is shorthand for `time.Now().Sub(t)`.

```go
func time.Sleep(d time.Duration)
```
Pauses the current goroutine for at least the duration `d`. A negative or zero duration causes `Sleep` to return immediately.

```go
func time.NewTicker(d time.Duration) *time.Ticker
```
Returns a new `Ticker` containing a channel that will send the current time on the channel after each tick. The ticker will adjust the intervals or drop ticks to make up for slow receivers.

```go
func time.After(d time.Duration) <-chan time.Time
```
Waits for the duration to elapse and then sends the current time on the returned channel. The underlying `Timer` is not recovered by the garbage collector until the timer fires.

```go
func time.Parse(layout, value string) (time.Time, error)
```
Parses a formatted string and returns the time value it represents. The layout defines the format by showing how the reference time `Mon Jan 2 15:04:05 -0700 MST 2006` would be represented.

```go
func sort.Slice(x any, less func(i, j int) bool)
```
Sorts the slice `x` given the provided `less` function. The sort is not guaranteed to be stable. `less` reports whether `x[i]` should be ordered before `x[j]`.

```go
func sort.SliceStable(x any, less func(i, j int) bool)
```
Sorts the slice `x` using the provided `less` function, keeping equal elements in their original order.

```go
func sort.Search(n int, f func(int) bool) int
```
Uses binary search to find the smallest index `i` in `[0, n)` at which `f(i)` is true, assuming `f(i) == true` implies `f(i+1) == true`.

```go
func slices.Sort[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S)
```
(Go 1.21+) Sorts a slice of any ordered type in ascending order. The sort is not guaranteed to be stable.

```go
func slices.Contains[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S, v E) bool
```
(Go 1.21+) Reports whether `v` is present in `s`.

```go
func slices.Index[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S, v E) int
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns the index of the first occurrence of `v` in `s`, or -1 if not present.

```go
func slices.SortFunc[S ~[]E, E any](x S, cmp func(a, b E) int)
```
(Go 1.21+) Sorts the slice `x` in ascending order as determined by the `cmp` function. The `cmp` function should return a negative number, zero, or a positive number.

```go
func slices.Compact[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S) S
```
(Go 1.21+) Replaces consecutive runs of equal elements with a single copy. This is like the `uniq` command on a sorted slice. Modifies the contents of the slice `s`.

```go
func maps.Keys[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) []K
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns the keys of the map `m`. The keys will be in an indeterminate order.

```go
func maps.Values[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) []V
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns the values of the map `m`. The values will be in an indeterminate order.

```go
func maps.Clone[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) M
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns a copy of `m`. This is a shallow clone: the new keys and values are set using ordinary assignment.

```go
func slog.Info(msg string, args ...any)
```
(Go 1.21+) Logs at `LevelInfo` on the default logger. Arguments are handled in key-value pairs: `slog.Info("msg", "key1", val1, "key2", val2)`.

```go
func slog.Error(msg string, args ...any)
```
(Go 1.21+) Logs at `LevelError` on the default logger.

```go
func slog.Debug(msg string, args ...any)
```
(Go 1.21+) Logs at `LevelDebug` on the default logger.

```go
func slog.Warn(msg string, args ...any)
```
(Go 1.21+) Logs at `LevelWarn` on the default logger.

```go
func slog.With(args ...any) *slog.Logger
```
(Go 1.21+) Returns a `Logger` that includes the given attributes in each output operation. Arguments are converted to `Attr` as if by `Logger.Log`.

```go
func log.Println(v ...any)
```
Calls `Output` to print to the standard logger. Arguments are handled in the manner of `fmt.Println`. Includes timestamp prefix.

```go
func log.Fatal(v ...any)
```
Equivalent to `Print()` followed by a call to `os.Exit(1)`.

```go
func log.Printf(format string, v ...any)
```
Calls `fmt.Sprintf` to format its arguments and calls `Output` to print to the standard logger.

```go
func regexp.Compile(expr string) (*regexp.Regexp, error)
```
Parses a regular expression and returns, if successful, a `Regexp` object that can be used to match against text.

```go
func regexp.MustCompile(str string) *regexp.Regexp
```
Like `Compile` but panics if the expression cannot be parsed. It simplifies safe initialization of global variables holding compiled regular expressions.

```go
func filepath.Join(elem ...string) string
```
Joins any number of path elements into a single path, separating them with an OS specific separator. Empty elements are ignored.

```go
func bufio.NewScanner(r io.Reader) *bufio.Scanner
```
Returns a new `Scanner` to read from `r`. The split function defaults to `ScanLines`. Call `Scan()` to advance, `Text()` to get the current token.

```go
func bufio.NewReader(rd io.Reader) *bufio.Reader
```
Returns a new `Reader` whose buffer has the default size (4096 bytes).
